integration_hint: At integration time, compute the oldest and latest email timestamps from the active Enron slice and keep only financial_snapshots and public_news_events whose dates overlap that email window.

financial_snapshots.label: FY1998 selected financial data
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $31.26B, net income was $703M, total assets were $29.35B, shareholders' equity was $7.05B, and diluted EPS was $1.01.

financial_snapshots.label: FY1999 selected financial data
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $40.11B, net income was $893M, total assets were $33.38B, shareholders' equity was $9.57B, and diluted EPS was $1.10.

financial_snapshots.label: Q4 1999 annual earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron reported full-year 1999 revenues of $40.0B, net income of $957M before nonrecurring items, annual diluted EPS of $1.18, and fourth-quarter diluted EPS of $0.31.

financial_snapshots.label: Q1 2000 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron reported first-quarter revenues of $13.1B, net income of $338M, and diluted EPS of $0.40.

financial_snapshots.label: Q2 2000 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron reported second-quarter revenues of $16.9B, net income of $289M, and diluted EPS of $0.34.

financial_snapshots.label: Q3 2000 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron reported third-quarter revenues of $30.0B, net income of $292M, and diluted EPS of $0.34.

financial_snapshots.label: FY2000 Form 10-K
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $100.79B, net income was $979M, total assets were $65.50B, shareholders' equity was $11.47B, and diluted EPS was $1.12.

financial_snapshots.label: Q4 2000 annual earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron publicly reported recurring annual diluted EPS of $1.47 for 2000 and fourth-quarter diluted EPS of $0.41.

financial_snapshots.label: Q1 2001 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron publicly reported first-quarter diluted EPS of $0.47, revenues of $50.1B, and net income of $406M.

financial_snapshots.label: Q2 2001 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron publicly reported second-quarter diluted EPS of $0.45 and net income of $404M.

financial_snapshots.label: Q3 2001 loss announcement
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron announced $1.01B of after-tax non-recurring charges, which resulted in a net loss for the quarter.

public_news_events.headline: Enron launched its first online emissions allowance auction
public_news_events.summary: Enron said EnronOnline would host the first online sulfur dioxide emissions allowance auction and planned to run the auctions monthly.

public_news_events.headline: Enron signed a $610M long-term electricity agreement with IBM
public_news_events.summary: Enron Energy Services said it would supply or procure electricity for IBM under a ten-year agreement valued at $610M.

public_news_events.headline: Enron announced a cash offer for MG plc
public_news_events.summary: Enron said its recommended cash offer valued MG plc at $446M and would extend Enron's business model into metals.

public_news_events.headline: EnronOnline passed $50B of transaction value in 2000
public_news_events.summary: Enron said EnronOnline had exceeded $50B of transaction value in calendar 2000 and had already beaten its yearly projection.

public_news_events.headline: Enron and Blockbuster announced a 20-year on-demand service deal
public_news_events.summary: Enron Broadband Services and Blockbuster announced a 20-year exclusive agreement to launch an entertainment-on-demand service.

public_news_events.headline: Enron launched Clickpaper.com for the forest products market
public_news_events.summary: Enron Industrial Markets launched Clickpaper.com as an Internet transaction system for pulp, paper, and wood commodities.

public_news_events.headline: PG&E entered Chapter 11
public_news_events.summary: PG&E's Chapter 11 filing made California power-counterparty risk a public fact before later Enron branch points tied to power trading and structured deals.

public_news_events.headline: Vice chairman Cliff Baxter resigned
public_news_events.summary: Enron's archived 2001 press chronology records Cliff Baxter's resignation on May 2, 2001.

public_news_events.headline: FERC imposed mitigation and opened the California refund path
public_news_events.summary: Public reporting on the June 19, 2001 FERC action put Enron and other sellers on notice that California crisis sales would face a refund and mitigation regime.

public_news_events.headline: Jeff Skilling resigned as chief executive
public_news_events.summary: Enron announced Skilling's resignation for personal reasons and said Kenneth Lay would resume the president and chief executive role.

public_news_events.headline: Enron announced a third-quarter loss
public_news_events.summary: The company disclosed $1.01B in after-tax non-recurring charges and said the quarter ended in a net loss.

public_news_events.headline: SEC requested information about related-party transactions
public_news_events.summary: Enron said the SEC had asked it to voluntarily provide information about certain related-party transactions.

public_news_events.headline: Moody's kept Enron under review for downgrade
public_news_events.summary: Public credit-rating reporting showed Enron's investment-grade status was under acute pressure as liquidity concerns deepened.

public_news_events.headline: Board formed a special committee and the SEC opened a formal investigation
public_news_events.summary: Enron said its board appointed a special committee and that the SEC had opened a formal investigation into the matters under review.

public_news_events.headline: S&P lowered Enron and kept it on CreditWatch negative
public_news_events.summary: S&P cut Enron's rating while leaving it just above junk, making the company's rating threshold a visible public constraint.

public_news_events.headline: Enron said it would restate prior financial statements
public_news_events.summary: Enron said it would restate results from 1997 through 2000 and the first two quarters of 2001 and reflect a $1.2B reduction in shareholders' equity.

public_news_events.headline: Enron announced a proposed sale to Dynegy
public_news_events.summary: Enron reported that Dynegy would acquire the company, subject to shareholder approval and other closing conditions.

public_news_events.headline: Moody's and S&P cut Enron to junk
public_news_events.summary: The final investment-grade break made clear that Enron's trading model could not survive a junk rating.

public_news_events.headline: Enron entered Chapter 11 proceedings
public_news_events.summary: The SEC proceeding record is used as the official source reference for Enron's Chapter 11 case.

public_news_events.headline: Sherron Watkins's memo became public
public_news_events.summary: Congressional investigators' release put the full warning memo into the public record months after the internal August 2001 warning.

public_news_events.headline: Arthur Andersen was indicted for obstruction
public_news_events.summary: The Justice Department unsealed the indictment of Enron's auditor, turning the document-destruction scandal into a formal criminal case.
